name: ccimport

metadata.name: ccimport
metadata.summary: a tiny package for fast python c++ binding build.

documentation:
# ccimport

[![Build Status](https://github.com/FindDefinition/ccimport/workflows/build/badge.svg)](https://github.com/FindDefinition/ccimport/actions?query=workflow%3Abuild)

a tiny package for fast python c++ binding build.

ccimport 0.2.x support python 3.5.
ccimport >= 0.3 support python 3.6-3.10.

## Usage

```Python
build_meta = ccimport.BuildMeta()
build_meta.add_global_includes(...)
build_meta.add_global_cflags(...)
build_meta.add_ldflags(...)

lib = ccimport([path1, path2], out_path, build_meta)
```

## Usage in setup.py 

see [example](example/setup)

functions.name: ccimport.loader.locate_package
documentation: locale package by find_spec, perfered method.

functions.name: ccimport.loader.locate_top_package
documentation:
you need to provide a setup.py in your project and
install it with pip install -e . or regular approach.
if a package is located via cwd, a warn is produced and cwd package
is returned.

functions.name: ccimport.loader.try_capture_import_parts
documentation:
if your function path is project/mod0/func_mod.py,
then the import_parts is [project, mod0, func_mod]

functions.name: ccimport.loader.try_import_from_path
documentation:
we try to find a valid package that contains the file
and use regular approach to import it.
if not found, just use file import.

modules.name: ccimport.ccpkg
documentation: JSON[header=True]

modules.name: ccimport.core
documentation:
a small library to replace cppimport.
when use in windows, you need to run ccimport/autoimport in vs developer prompt.
example config in microsoft terminal:

{
    "name" : "Developer Command Prompt for VS 2019",
    "commandline" : "powershell.exe -noe -c "&{Import-Module """C:/Program Files (x86)/Microsoft Visual Studio/2019/Community/Common7/Tools/Microsoft.VisualStudio.DevShell.dll"""; Enter-VsDevShell 4bd12d00 -DevCmdArguments '-arch=x64 -no_logo'}"",
    "startingDirectory" : "%USERPROFILE%"
}

functions.name: ccimport.buildmeta.group_dict_by_split
documentation:
convert {gcc,clang++: [xxx], clang++: [yyy]}
to {gcc: [xxx], clang++: [xxx, yyy]}

methods.name: ccimport.buildmeta.BuildMeta.__init__
documentation: TODO add private flags

modules.name: ccimport.source_iter
documentation:
small library to find classes/functions (need macro).
libclang is too huge for simple c++ analysis.

methods.name: ccimport.source_iter.CppSourceIterator.find_all_class_def
documentation:
1. find class keyword
2. try to find next '{' or ';'. if '{', it's a class def, else is a declare.
3. find prev 'template' or '}' or ';' to determine if is a class template

functions.name: ccimport.compat._compiler_preprocessor_verbose
documentation:
Capture the compiler preprocessor stage in verbose mode
copied from https://github.com/AndrewWalker/ccsyspath/blob/master/ccsyspath/paths.py

functions.name: ccimport.compat.locate_libpython_2
documentation:
Get path to the python library associated with the current python
interpreter.

functions.name: ccimport.buildtools.writer._override_flags
documentation:
if a flag exists in _ALL_OVERRIDE_FLAGS, the one
in major flag will override the one in minor.

functions.name: ccimport.buildtools.writer.fill_build_flags
documentation: fill compile/link compiler-to-list flags with all compiler.

functions.name: ccimport.buildtools.writer.fill_link_flags
documentation: fill compile/link compiler-to-list flags with all compiler.
